bullets.text: The EU has approved a €90bn loan for Ukraine, following the reopening of the Druzhba pipeline, which had been stalled for months over oil supplies to Hungary.
Democracy is increasingly threatened by the climate crisis, with a new report indicating that natural hazards linked to climate change disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024.
The Swiss National Bank has cut interest rates by a half point to 0.5%, while Federal Reserve Chair Powell indicated that a rate cut could be 'on the table' as soon as September.
Iran has reportedly seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz, with the White House stating that the US is satisfied with the blockade. This comes amidst a ceasefire that has turned chaotic.

Concerns are growing over potential cuts to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), with families fearing financial hardship and a reduction in support for those with disabilities, particularly children and adults with autism.
A small plane in Los Angeles clipped power lines and crashed into a car park, leading to the rescue of the pilot.
Iran has reportedly seized two container ships in the Strait of Hormuz, causing oil prices to rise above $100 a barrel and contributing to market volatility.
Tesla's shares rallied after its first-quarter earnings beat expectations, with net income rising 17% from last year's lows, despite being its second-weakest figure in five years.
Switzerland has proposed a $20 billion capital increase for UBS as part of banking reforms, following months of lobbying by the country's largest lender.
